tools.name: search_memories
tools.description: Searches semantically across stored memories using Mem0. Use this to retrieve relevant context about user preferences, patterns, and past interactions before responding. This is for retrieval only - use add_memory to store new memories, and list_memories to see all memories.

query.description: Search query text to find relevant memories

limit.description: Maximum number of memories to return

user.description: User ID to search memories for (defaults to configured user)

tools.name: add_memory
tools.description: Adds new memories to Mem0 storage. Use this when user explicitly asks to remember something or to store conversation context for future retrieval. Supports two forms: simple text memory or conversation messages (auto-extracts memories via LLM). For searching stored memories, use search_memories instead.

text.description: Simple text memory to store (use instead of messages)

messages.description: Array of conversation messages to extract memories from (use instead of text)

user.description: User ID to store memory for (defaults to configured user)

tools.name: list_memories
tools.description: Lists all stored memories for a user with IDs and creation dates. Use this to see what memories are currently stored. For searching specific memories, use search_memories instead.

user.description: User ID to list memories for (defaults to configured user)

tools.name: delete_memory
tools.description: Deletes memories from Mem0 storage. Use this to remove specific memories or all memories for a user. Supports two modes: delete a specific memory by ID, or delete all memories for a user.

memory_id.description: ID of specific memory to delete (use instead of all flag)

all.description: Delete all memories for the user (use instead of memory_id)

user.description: User ID to delete memories for (required when using all flag)

messages.content: Hey, I'm curious about something. Can you show me what film preferences and patterns you've already stored in my memory? I want to see what you remember about my tastes before we continue our conversation.
I'll check your stored memories to see what film preferences and patterns I've saved for you.

The results show that there are currently **no film preferences or patterns stored** in your memory. Both the list of all memories and a specific search for film/movie-related memories came back empty.

This means we haven't yet discussed your movie tastes, or you haven't asked me to remember any specific preferences yet.

If you'd like, we can start building up your film profile now. What are some of your favorite genres, movies, or directors? I can remember these for future conversations if you'd like!
Could you search for any film preferences or recommendations related to sci-fi? I'd like to see if anything about that genre is stored in my memory.

Nothing came up for sci-fi either — the search returned empty results. 

So far, your memory is a blank slate when it comes to film preferences, including sci-fi. If you'd like, I can start remembering your sci-fi favorites (or any other movie preferences) as we discuss them!

That sounds great! I've always been a big fan of sci-fi, especially the classics like Blade Runner and The Matrix. I also really enjoy thought-provoking films with complex narratives. Could you remember that I prefer sci-fi movies that explore philosophical themes?

function_call.name: skill-tools-add_memory
function_call.arguments: {"text": "User's film preferences: Fan of sci-fi, especially classics like Blade Runner and The Matrix. Enjoys thought-provoking films with complex narratives. Prefers sci-fi movies that explore philosophical themes.", "user": "default"}

messages.content: {"memory": {"id": "m2", "user": "default", "text": "User's film preferences: Fan of sci-fi, especially classics like Blade Runner and The Matrix. Enjoys thought-provoking films with complex narratives. Prefers sci-fi movies that explore philosophical themes."}}
messages.name: skill-tools-add_memory

messages.content:
Done! I've stored your film preferences in memory. Here's what I'll remember for you:

- **Favorite genre**: Sci-fi, especially classics
- **Favorite films**: Blade Runner and The Matrix
- **Preferred style**: Thought-provoking films with complex narratives
- **Theme preference**: Sci-fi movies that explore philosophical themes

Now I have a record of your tastes for future conversations. If you discover more favorites or want to add other preferences (other genres, directors, etc.), just let me know!
Thanks for saving that! Could you double-check that my sci-fi preferences were stored correctly? I want to make sure everything is there for our next conversation.

function_call.name: skill-tools-search_memories
function_call.arguments: {"limit": 5, "query": "sci-fi Blade Runner The Matrix philosophical themes film preferences", "user": "default"}

> "User's film preferences: Fan of sci-fi, especially classics like Blade Runner and The Matrix. Enjoys thought-provoking films with complex narratives. Prefers sci-fi movies that explore philosophical themes."

Everything is there and ready for our next conversation! 🎬
